one_liner: TTD at $19.75 has a confirmed VCP (pivot $24.32), 70% DCF upside, and is building above EMA50 on volume drying — programmatic advertising recovery thesis intact ahead of August 6 earnings.

price_action.vol_rank: Well below average — very low volume on a +3.57% day
price_action.intraday_story: TTD opened at $18.60, ran strongly to $19.80, closing at $19.75 — a solid day. But volume at 0.43x average is very low, meaning the move lacked participation. A low-volume rally after a base is constructive but needs volume confirmation to signal the breakout.

news_catalyst.headline: No TTD-Specific News — Recovering with Ad Market Sentiment
news_catalyst.summary: No Trade Desk-specific news. The stock is recovering with broader ad market sentiment as digital advertising demand remains solid heading into H2. The VCP pivot at $24.32 is the technical target; earnings August 6 will be the fundamental catalyst.

technical.summary: TTD has a confirmed VCP with pivot at $24.32. Price is above EMA50 ($18.98) and EMA200 ($19.18) — healthy structure. RSI 60.9 is in bullish territory. Volume at 0.43x average is drying — the key accumulation signal. Wyckoff support $17.46, resistance $24.73. The VCP thesis requires a volume-expanding break above $24.32 to confirm.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: VCP pivot at $24.32 is the watchlist target. Low-volume consolidation above both EMAs is the setup. Break above $19.80 (session high) on 1.5x+ volume is the first sign of strength.

valuation.summary: DCF $33.51 vs $19.75 — 70% upside. Grade B (0.68) fundamentals are the weakest link in the accumulate thesis but DCF margin of safety is large.

key_strengths: Market-leading DSP with CTV advertising exposure
Kokai AI platform driving advertiser ROI improvements
key_concerns: Grade B (0.676) — weakest B on watchlist
CTV competition from Google and Amazon intensifying
fundamentals.summary: B grade with lower end score. DCF upside is the primary bull case, supported by programmatic ad market recovery.

risks: Volume at 0.43x average — needs confirmation; low-volume rallies can reverse quickly
VCP pivot at $24.32 is 23% away — significant work needed to get there
August 6 earnings risk — miss on CTV advertising growth would break the base
